headlineOverride: Capital attention stays concentrated in AI infrastructure, grid buildout, and reserve diversification.
heroQuestions: What is capital paying up for right now?
Which variables are driving cross-asset resonance?
Where is consensus crowded and where is divergence still investable?

themes.name: AI Capex
themes.description: Compute bottlenecks and hyperscaler spend keep AI infrastructure at the top of the attention stack.

themes.name: Grid Buildout
themes.description: Power demand from data centers and electrification is pulling grid investment into the core macro conversation.

variables.name: Power Demand
variables.description: Electricity demand from AI data centers and electrification is tightening capacity planning.
variables.currentState: Utilities and equipment vendors are repricing long-cycle demand visibility.
variables.pricingPath: Higher demand supports utility capex, transformer orders, and regional power scarcity trades.
variables.riskStatement: A slower deployment pace or faster supply response would reduce the scarcity premium.

variables.name: US Real Rates
variables.description: Real yields still shape discount rates and valuation tolerance across long-duration themes.
variables.currentState: Markets remain sensitive to inflation surprises and policy repricing.
variables.pricingPath: Higher real rates tighten valuation multiples and change the hurdle rate for duration-heavy themes.
variables.riskStatement: A faster disinflation path would soften the headwind and reopen multiple expansion.

variables.name: Copper
variables.description: Copper reflects the market's view on grid buildout and industrial electrification.
variables.currentState: Supply discipline and electrification demand keep the medium-term setup constructive.
variables.pricingPath: A tighter copper market strengthens the capex narrative around grids and power infrastructure.
variables.riskStatement: A sharp global growth slowdown would weaken demand expectations and compress the thesis.

reports.title: AI Infrastructure: Bottlenecks Still Binding
reports.summary: Infrastructure constraints continue to anchor pricing power across semiconductors and power equipment.

reports.title: Reserve Diversification Still Supports Gold
reports.summary: Reserve managers remain active and keep strategic demand under the gold complex.

logicChains.title: AI demand meets infrastructure scarcity
logicChains.trigger: Hyperscaler capex guidance stays strong while supply remains constrained.
logicChains.firstOrderEffect: Critical suppliers retain pricing power and backlog visibility.
logicChains.secondOrderEffect: Power infrastructure, cooling, and regional utilities move into the same attention cluster.
logicChains.falsification: New supply arrives faster than demand or hyperscaler spending decelerates materially.

logicChains.title: Electricity demand drives grid capex
logicChains.trigger: Data center and electrification demand exceed current grid capacity.
logicChains.firstOrderEffect: Utilities accelerate transmission and transformer investment.
logicChains.secondOrderEffect: Industrial suppliers and selected materials gain earnings visibility.
logicChains.falsification: Demand ramps slower or permitting and policy support weaken.

divergencePoints.title: Can AI capex outrun valuation pressure?
divergencePoints.bullCase: Structural demand and scarcity keep earnings upgrades ahead of multiple compression.
divergencePoints.bearCase: Crowded positioning and rate pressure cap upside even with strong fundamentals.
divergencePoints.whatBreaksTie: The next earnings cycle needs to show both utilization strength and supply discipline.

decisionMemo.headline: Capital attention remains concentrated in infrastructure scarcity themes.
decisionMemo.summary: AI infrastructure and grid buildout still dominate attention because investors are paying for scarcity, backlog visibility, and cross-asset reinforcement. Real rates remain the key valuation filter.
watchItems: Monitor utility lead times and transformer backlog commentary.
Watch real-rate repricing after inflation data.
candidateExpressions: Long power infrastructure beneficiaries against neutral broad cyclicals.
Prefer scarcity suppliers over downstream adopters exposed to input constraints.
invalidations: A material slowdown in hyperscaler capex.
A fast easing in supply bottlenecks across compute and power equipment.
